34.reason: [Should be 'else if' to avoid checking n == 0 after handling n == 1; if input is 1, both blocks may execute incorrectly]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be 'else if' to avoid checking n == 0 after handling n == 1; if input is 1, both blocks may execute incorrectly]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Logic error: should be 'else if' to avoid checking n==0 after handling n==1, because if n==1, this block will also execute incorrectly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Should be 'else if' to avoid checking n==0 after handling n==1; otherwise, when n==1, it will also fall through and check n==0, which is incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Should be 'else if' to avoid checking n == 0 after handling n == 1; otherwise, if n == 1, it will also check n == 0 condition incorrectly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [This should be 'else if (n == 0)' to avoid checking for pop when n == 1. As written, after handling a push (n == 1), the code will also check the pop condition (n == 0), which is false, so it may not cause immediate harm, but if n were both 1 and 0 (impossible), it's logically incorrect. However, more critically, when n == -1, this 'if' block is skipped, but the 'else break;' belongs to the second 'if', not the first. So if n == 1, after push, it checks second if (false), then skips else. If n == 0, handles pop, skips else. But if n == -1, first if false, second if false, so executes 'else break;' 鈥 which is correct. So control flow is actually okay, but using 'else if' is clearer and safer. However, the real issue is uninitialized sta.top.]
